memorydb.subnetgroup.CfnAbout: Specifies a subnet group. A subnet group is a collection of subnets (typically private) that you can designate for your clusters running in an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) environment. When you create a cluster in an Amazon VPC, you must specify a subnet group. MemoryDB uses that subnet group to choose a subnet and IP addresses within that subnet to associate with your nodes. For more information, see [Subnets and subnet groups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/memorydb/latest/devguide/subnetgroups.html).

memorydb.subnetgroup.Description.Description: A description of the subnet group.

memorydb.subnetgroup.SubnetGroupName.Description: The name of the subnet group to be used for the cluster.

memorydb.subnetgroup.SubnetIds.Description: A list of Amazon VPC subnet IDs for the subnet group.

memorydb.subnetgroup.Tags.Description: An array of key-value pairs to apply to this resource.  For more information, see [Tag](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-resource-tags.html).
